cards.back: A type of neural network that is trained to reconstruct its input, often used for dimensionality reduction and feature learning
A regularization technique that randomly sets a fraction of neurons to zero during training, preventing overfitting
A technique that normalizes the input of each layer to have a mean of 0 and a standard deviation of 1, improving training speed and stability
Rectified Linear Unit, a widely used activation function that maps all negative values to 0 and all positive values to the same value
A technique that artificially increases the size of a dataset by applying random transformations to the existing images, improving model generalizability
